reason: Hook output and fail-open behavior remain the highest-risk contract during the runtime rewrite.
The migration touches retry behavior and persistence, so execution needs the backend verification checklist.
Hook wiring must stay declarative and relocatable when switching from Python to Node.
Docs, manifest metadata, and hook wiring all change together and must be reviewed as one user-facing surface.
The archived implementation plan captures the prior test and validation expectations that the JS rewrite should keep or replace explicitly.
